title: End-to-End Training
concept: End-to-end training allows the backbone, RPN, classifier, and regressor to be optimized simultaneously using a single backpropagation pipeline.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What layers make up the shared backbone?

options.text: The convolutional layers of the feature extractor (like ResNet)

exercises.explain: The backbone refers to the initial convolutional feature extraction layers.

cards.title: A Single Training Session
cards.body: If you are learning to play basketball, you don't spend a month only practicing running, then a month only practicing passing, then a month only practicing shooting, without playing a game. You practice them together. End-to-end training allows the network to learn all its tasks together.

cards.title: End-to-End Training
cards.body: End-to-End Training is the optimization methodology where all modules of the neural network (backbone, proposal network, classification, and regression heads) are trained simultaneously using a single unified loss function and backpropagation.

cards.title: The Symphony Orchestra
cards.body: Instead of training the woodwinds, strings, and brass in separate rooms and hoping they play well together, the conductor trains the entire orchestra in the same hall. The musicians adjust their timing and volume in response to each other in real-time.

cards.title: The Unified Loss Function
cards.body: Faster R-CNN combines 4 losses: 1) RPN objectness loss, 2) RPN coordinate offset loss, 3) Final classification loss, and 4) Final box offset loss. Gradients from all 4 losses flow backward through the network in a single pass.

cards.title: No complex multi-stage setups
cards.body: It's a myth that you must train the RPN first, freeze it, and then train the classifier. While early papers used a complex 4-step alternating training scheme, modern deep learning frameworks optimize the entire network jointly in a single training step.

cards.title: Unified Backpropagation
cards.body: Visualizing the gradient flow in end-to-end training:

left.text: Image -> Backbone -> RPN -> RoI Pooling -> Classification & Regression.

right.text: Losses calculated -> Gradients flow back -> Updates all weights at once.

exercises.prompt: What does end-to-end training mean for Faster R-CNN?
exercises.explain: End-to-end training optimizes all network components together using backpropagation of a combined loss function.

options.text: The entire network (backbone, RPN, classifier, regressor) is trained together in a single optimization pipeline
The training process starts at the end of the dataset and runs backwards
The model is trained only on CPU hardware
Only the classification head weights are updated

exercises.prompt: True or False: In modern training pipelines, the RPN is trained in a separate Python script from the final detection head.
exercises.explain: False. Modern frameworks train both the RPN and the detection heads together in the same training loop.
A unified (or combined) multi-task loss function allows all parameters to be optimized in one step.
exercises.sentence: End-to-end training is enabled by combining RPN losses and final detection losses into a single ___ loss function.

exercises.prompt: Which training flow represents a fully end-to-end system?
exercises.explain: Updating all network weights in a single backpropagation step represents end-to-end training.

options.text: A single optimizer step updating the backbone and all output heads simultaneously based on a joint loss
Extracting features to disk, and then training an SVM classifier next week
Manually tuning thresholds for color grouping

exercises.prompt: You are training Faster R-CNN. If the RPN is making poor region proposals, how does end-to-end training resolve this?

options.text: The high proposal loss calculates large gradients that backpropagate all the way to the backbone, forcing the network to adjust features and propose better boxes.
The user must manually adjust the anchor boxes.
The classifier ignores the poor proposals.
The network freezes training for that epoch.

exercises.explain: Exactly! The joint loss ensures that if any part of the network (like the RPN) struggles, its feedback guides weight updates throughout the entire network. Next: let's compare the neural RPN against classical Selective Search!
